source_quote: At 6 months post-intervention, COPE teens had a lower mean BMI than teens in Healthy Teens (COPE=24.72, Healthy Teens=25.05, adjusted M= −0.34, 95% CI= −0.56, −0.11).
reasoning: The outcome is 'BMI short term'. The results_text explicitly states at 6 months post-intervention the adjusted mean difference is −0.34 (95% CI −0.56, −0.11), favoring COPE (lower BMI). This is a direct between-group comparison with point estimate and confidence interval.

source_quote: These ﬁndings were consistent for weight (−0.62 kg (−1.21, 0.03), P = 0.01) and BMI (−0.28 kg m −2 (−0.49, −0.06), P = 0.01) at 24 months
reasoning: The outcome is 'BMI medium term'. The results_text reports group-by-time effects at 24 months for BMI: −0.28 kg/m² (95% CI −0.49, −0.06), P = 0.01, favoring the intervention group. This is the medium-term outcome (24 months).

source_quote: Boys BMI† 23.3 (3.5) control vs 19.7 (3.1) intervention, Differencea +3.6, 95% Confidence Limits Min +2.12 Max +5.07, P-value <0.05
reasoning: The outcome is 'BMI short term'. Table 2 shows BMI after 3 months for boys: Control mean 23.3 (SD 3.5), Intervention mean 19.7 (SD 3.1). The difference (control minus intervention) is +3.6 (95% CI 2.12, 5.07, p<0.05). This indicates intervention group had significantly lower BMI. Note: this is control-intervention difference (positive means control higher).
